notes: Flask benchmark annotations. Baseline from SUMMARY.md (2026-03-03). TPs/FPs verified during Mag7 review.

true_positives.note: Intentional exec() for config loading — TP but acceptable risk
Intentional eval() for config loading — TP but acceptable risk
Bare except intentionally catches BaseException (including SystemExit/KeyboardInterrupt) to record error before re-raising — deliberate but genuinely catches too broadly
Flask's App class is genuinely large by design — valid code smell even if it's an accepted architectural choice

false_positives.note: This is just an initializer; load_app() checks for None before returning and sets it via the loader path
Initialization to None is intentional; _cv_token is set in push() and checked before use in pop()
Docstring explicitly documents it may be None; callers are expected to check — this is intentional API design
data is initialized as {} on line 745 and only updated via |= — it is always a dict, never None
None means 'not yet loaded from session'; callers check for None before use — this is a lazy-load sentinel
This is CLI-only code (flask command); traceback goes to the developer's terminal, not to HTTP clients
CLI run command during reloader — prints to developer's stderr, not exposed to HTTP clients
CLI error output (err=True goes to stderr) shown to developer, not to HTTP clients
PYTHONSTARTUP is a standard Python env var; Flask mirrors CPython's own behavior — if env is compromised, game is already over
PYTHONSTARTUP is standard CPython behavior; env vars are trusted in this context (developer's own shell session)
ValueError means invalid sort key was passed; graceful fallback to unsorted output is intentional CLI UX
Intentional fallback: try to parse env var as JSON, keep as string if it fails — comment documents the intent
module_name comes from the developer's CLI --app argument, not from untrusted input; this is core app-loading logic
import_name is the app's own package name — developer-controlled, used to resolve resource paths
CLI startup loading .env files — no security boundary; race is theoretical and inconsequential here
Developer-local PYTHONSTARTUP check; TOCTOU race is not exploitable since env is already trusted
Mirrors CPython's own PYTHONSTARTUP handling in the interactive shell — input is developer-controlled, not user-controlled
Standard pattern for optional imports — readline may not be available on all platforms (e.g., Windows)
Adds the project directory to sys.path for app discovery — standard Flask CLI behavior, path is the developer's own project
This is a decorator override that delegates to super().command() — forwarding args/kwargs is inherent to the pattern, not feature envy
This IS the flask shell command — an interactive REPL is the entire purpose of this function
Debug helper that swaps in an enhanced dict subclass for better error messages — only active in debug mode, not a security issue
Standard template loader chain pattern — try each loader, continue to next on miss, raise after all exhausted
Standard module-level caching pattern for a version string — no mutation risk, simple lazy initialization

expected_counts.notes: Expected counts from Round 2 FP reduction (SUMMARY.md)
